name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description: Project Layout is a Java utility library for detecting, describing, and working with conventional on-disk project directory layouts in a consistent way. It provides a central abstraction (org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) that models a project rooted at a filesystem directory and exposes root-relative paths for production sources, tests, and documentation, plus optional module discovery. The library includes multiple layout implementations that infer structure from build metadata or common conventions: MavenProjectLayout reads pom.xml (via PomReader) to determine multi-module structure (packaging=pom), source/resource directories, test directories, and documentation inputs (src/site); GragleProjectLayout uses the Gradle Tooling API to load a GradleProject model, derive child module names, and returns conventional directories (src/main, src/test, src/site); JScriptProjectLayout parses package.json workspaces and resolves workspace module directories by matching glob patterns to folders containing package.json; PythonProjectLayout detects Python projects using pyproject.toml metadata; DefaultProjectLayout provides a minimal filesystem-based fallback that treats immediate subdirectories as modules (excluding tool/build directories). A higher-level orchestrator (org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager) detects the applicable layout (Maven, Gradle, JS/TS, Python, or default) for a given project directory, and org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor provides a base workflow to scan a project, recurse into modules when present, and delegate per-folder processing to subclasses. Typical usage is to call ProjectLayoutManager.detectProjectLayout(java.io.File) to obtain a ProjectLayout, then query getModules(), getSources(), getTests(), and getDocuments() to drive tooling that needs reliable location of well-known folders across diverse projects.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Detects the appropriate ProjectLayout implementation for a project directory by checking for Maven (pom.xml), Gradle (build.gradle), JavaScript/TypeScript (package.json), Python (pyproject.toml) and falling back to a filesystem-based DefaultProjectLayout.

examples.description: Detect a project layout for a given repository root.

features.name: getModules
features.description: Returns module directories or names when the layout can infer nested modules (e.g., Maven modules, Gradle children, npm/yarn workspaces, or immediate subdirectories for the default layout).

examples.description: Enumerate modules for a multi-module repository (when supported by the detected layout).

features.name: getSources
features.description: Returns conventional root-relative source directories for production code based on the detected build system or layout conventions (e.g., Maven build sourceDirectory/resources or Gradle src/main).

examples.description: Get production source roots for a Maven project.

features.name: scanFolder
features.description: Scans a project directory, detects its ProjectLayout, and either processes modules recursively or processes the root folder directly when modules are not reported by the layout.

examples.description: Implement a processor that uses detected layout paths while scanning a repository.
